title: Passport is a Travel Document, Not Proof of Citizenship: MEA on Passport Seva Divas
summary: A senior Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) official clarified on Passport Seva Divas (June 24) that a passport is a travel document and does not constitute proof of citizenship. The statement is significant in the context of ongoing debates around the National Register of Citizens (NRC) and citizenship documentation. The Passport Act, 1967 governs the issuance of passports in India. Citizenship is determined under the Citizenship Act, 1955 and relevant constitutional provisions.
exam_angle: Connects to constitutional provisions on citizenship (Articles 5–11), the Citizenship Act 1955, and the distinction between travel documents and citizenship proof — highly relevant for Polity GS Paper 2 and current NRC debates.
key_facts: Statement made on Passport Seva Divas (June 24)
Clarification by: Ministry of External Affairs (MEA)
Passport governed by: Passport Act, 1967
Citizenship determined by: Citizenship Act, 1955 and Articles 5–11 of the Constitution

title: CRPF Suspends DIG-Rank Officer Over Messages Against CAPF Bill; 20 Others Transferred
summary: The Central Reserve Police Force (CRPF) suspended a DIG-rank officer for sharing messages allegedly against the Centre's Central Armed Police Forces (CAPF) Bill. Twenty other personnel were transferred after their family members staged protests against the proposed legislation. The CAPF Bill is reported to propose significant changes in service conditions, transfers, and postings of personnel in central paramilitary forces. The incident highlights tensions between the government's legislative agenda and paramilitary personnel.
exam_angle: Relevant for understanding the structure and governance of Central Armed Police Forces (CAPFs) under the Ministry of Home Affairs, and the legislative process for service condition reforms — GS Paper 2 (Polity/Governance) and GS Paper 3 (Internal Security).
key_facts: Suspended officer rank: DIG (Deputy Inspector General)
Force involved: Central Reserve Police Force (CRPF)
Trigger: Messages against the CAPF Bill
20 other officers transferred after family protests

title: India–US Trade Talks Conclude; Both Sides Note 'Substantial Progress' Toward Interim Deal
summary: US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer concluded a two-day visit to New Delhi, after which both India and the US noted 'substantial progress' in bilateral trade agreement talks. India said it discussed pathways to an interim trade deal with the US, with a target of finalising a first-phase deal before July 24, 2026. A senior US official described the two nations as 'very, very close' to a historic agreement. Talks covered tariff structures, market access, and sectoral issues including American whiskey tariffs in India.
exam_angle: Critical for GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Economy) — covers India-US trade relations, the Bilateral Trade Agreement (BTA) process, and tariff negotiations relevant to India's trade policy.
key_facts: US representative: USTR Jamieson Greer (two-day Delhi visit)
Target deadline for Phase-1 deal: July 24, 2026
Both sides described 'substantial progress'
Key issues: tariffs, market access, American whiskey import duties

title: Iran Assures US of Toll-Free Strait of Hormuz Passage Amid Nuclear Talks
summary: US President Donald Trump stated that Iran has assured the United States of toll-free passage through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global oil transit chokepoint. US Secretary of State Rubio confirmed that the US will not accept Iranian tolls on the Strait. Trump noted that 19 million barrels of oil flowed through the Strait of Hormuz on Monday, calling it an all-time record. The development comes amid ongoing US–Iran nuclear negotiations.
exam_angle: The Strait of Hormuz is a key chokepoint for global energy security; connects to GS Paper 2 (IR) topics on West Asia geopolitics, Iran nuclear deal, and India's energy import security.
key_facts: Strait of Hormuz: connects Persian Gulf to Gulf of Oman; ~20% of global oil passes through it
Iran gave assurances of toll-free passage to the US
19 million barrels of oil flowed through on a single day (record)
Context: ongoing US–Iran nuclear negotiations

title: Iran Invites PM Modi to Attend Supreme Leader Khamenei's Funeral
summary: Iran extended an invitation to Prime Minister Narendra Modi to attend the funeral of Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei. This diplomatic outreach reflects India's strong bilateral ties with Iran, which are strategically important for connectivity projects such as the Chabahar Port and the International North–South Transport Corridor (INSTC). India has traditionally maintained independent diplomatic engagement with Iran despite Western sanctions. A formal Indian government response to the invitation is awaited.
exam_angle: Tests knowledge of India–Iran relations, Chabahar Port (India's strategic asset), INSTC, and India's foreign policy of strategic autonomy — GS Paper 2 (IR).
key_facts: Iran invited PM Modi to attend Supreme Leader Khamenei's funeral
India–Iran ties: anchored by Chabahar Port and INSTC
India has maintained engagement with Iran independently of Western sanctions
Khamenei was Iran's Supreme Leader

title: Air India Delhi–Amritsar Flight Briefly Enters Pakistani Airspace; Pilot Taken Off Duty, Probe Ordered
summary: An Air India flight operating the Delhi–Amritsar route accidentally drifted into Pakistani airspace near Amritsar. The pilot has been taken off duty pending an investigation by the Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA). The incident is particularly sensitive given the suspended bilateral aviation arrangements between India and Pakistan following the 2025 Pahalgam terror attack. Both countries' aviation authorities are expected to examine the breach.
exam_angle: Connects to aviation regulation (DGCA), India–Pakistan relations, airspace sovereignty under ICAO norms, and the broader context of suspended bilateral ties — GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Internal Security).
key_facts: Flight: Air India, Delhi–Amritsar route
Incident: Brief entry into Pakistani airspace near Amritsar
Regulatory body: DGCA (Directorate General of Civil Aviation)
Pilot taken off duty; probe ordered

title: RBI Finalises Guidelines for Upper-Layer NBFCs; Raises Large Exposure Limit for NBFC-IFCs
summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has finalised guidelines for Non-Banking Financial Companies classified in the Upper Layer (NBFC-UL) under the Scale-Based Regulation (SBR) framework. The large exposure limit for NBFC-IFCs (Infrastructure Finance Companies) is being raised from 35% to 45% of the eligible capital base. The norms maintain ambiguity regarding the mandatory listing of Tata Sons, which was classified as an Upper Layer NBFC. The regulations are intended to bring greater systemic oversight to large NBFCs.
exam_angle: Directly tests RBI's Scale-Based Regulation framework for NBFCs, classification of NBFC-UL, and prudential norms — a recurring GS Paper 3 (Economy) and Prelims topic.
key_facts: RBI framework: Scale-Based Regulation (SBR) for NBFCs
NBFC-IFC large exposure limit: raised from 35% to 45% of eligible capital base
Tata Sons classified as Upper Layer NBFC; listing requirement still unclear
Regulator: Reserve Bank of India (RBI)

title: RBI Proposes Norms to Manage AI/ML-Related Risks for Regulated Entities
summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has proposed draft norms to manage risks arising from the use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) by its regulated entities, including banks and NBFCs. The framework aims to ensure responsible AI adoption, data governance, model risk management, and accountability. This is part of RBI's broader effort to keep pace with rapid technology adoption in the financial sector. Regulated entities will be required to put in place board-approved AI risk management policies.
exam_angle: Connects RBI's regulatory mandate with emerging technology governance — relevant for GS Paper 3 (Economy and Sci-Tech) and questions on fintech regulation in India.
key_facts: Proposed by: Reserve Bank of India (RBI)
Applies to: RBI-regulated entities (banks, NBFCs, etc.)
Focus: AI/ML risk management — data governance, model risk, accountability
Regulated entities to adopt board-approved AI risk policies

title: OpenAI and Broadcom Unveil Custom AI Chip 'Jalapeno' to Reduce Infrastructure Costs
summary: OpenAI, in partnership with semiconductor company Broadcom, unveiled its first custom AI chip named 'Jalapeno', designed to run AI models faster and more cost-effectively. The chip is part of OpenAI's strategy to build its own AI infrastructure stack and reduce dependence on third-party GPU providers like NVIDIA. Separately, Qualcomm announced a $3.9 billion acquisition of Modular, an AI infrastructure company. These developments signal a major shift in the global AI chip industry.
exam_angle: Relevant for GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech) — AI hardware, semiconductor geopolitics, and India's semiconductor mission context; also important for understanding global AI infrastructure competition.
key_facts: OpenAI's custom chip: named 'Jalapeno', developed with Broadcom
Purpose: faster, cheaper AI model inference
Qualcomm to acquire AI infrastructure firm Modular for $3.9 billion
Significance: reduces dependence on NVIDIA GPUs

title: Supreme Court Favours Lower NEET-SS Percentile Cutoff for In-Service Doctors
summary: The Supreme Court of India expressed support for maintaining a lower percentile cutoff in NEET-SS (National Eligibility cum Entrance Test for Super Speciality) for in-service doctors, observing that such doctors cannot leave their duties to study full-time like fresh candidates. The court noted the practical constraints faced by government doctors who serve in remote areas while attempting super-speciality entrance exams. A separate matter saw the SC issue notice to the National Medical Commission (NMC) on Tamil Nadu's plea against surrendering 152 super-speciality seats to the All India Quota.
exam_angle: Tests knowledge of NEET, the role of NMC (replacing MCI), All India Quota for medical admissions, and federalism tensions in medical education — GS Paper 2 (Governance/Polity).
key_facts: NEET-SS: entrance exam for super-speciality medical admissions
SC favours lower percentile for in-service (government) doctors
NMC issued notice on Tamil Nadu's plea against surrendering 152 super-speciality seats to AIQ
Regulatory body: National Medical Commission (NMC)

title: Supreme Court Slams CARA Over Adoption Delays; Issues Directive
summary: The Supreme Court of India strongly criticised the Central Adoption Resource Authority (CARA) over prolonged delays in processing adoptions, asking 'What's your problem?' in a sharp observation. CARA functions under the Ministry of Women and Child Development and is the nodal body for adoption of orphaned, abandoned, and surrendered children in India. The court's intervention follows persistent complaints about bureaucratic delays leaving children in institutional care for years. The SC is expected to issue directions to expedite the adoption process.
exam_angle: CARA, the Juvenile Justice (Care and Protection of Children) Act 2015, and adoption law are standard GS Paper 2 topics on social justice and governance.
key_facts: CARA: Central Adoption Resource Authority, under Ministry of Women and Child Development
Governing law: Juvenile Justice (Care and Protection of Children) Act, 2015
SC criticism: excessive delays in processing adoptions
CARA is the statutory nodal body for domestic and inter-country adoptions

title: NHAI Identifies 595 Accident-Prone Unauthorised Parking Spots on Highways After SC Order
summary: The National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) has identified 595 accident-prone, unauthorised parking locations on national highways following a Supreme Court directive. The SC had ordered a survey and remedial action after multiple fatal accidents involving vehicles parked on highway shoulders. NHAI is now expected to take enforcement action, including removal of encroachments and development of authorised parking facilities. This is part of broader road safety measures under the Motor Vehicles (Amendment) Act 2019.
exam_angle: Connects to road safety governance, NHAI's mandate, Supreme Court's role in executive oversight, and Motor Vehicles Act provisions — relevant for GS Paper 2 (Governance) and GS Paper 3 (Infrastructure).
key_facts: NHAI identified 595 accident-prone unauthorised parking areas
Action: pursuant to Supreme Court directive
Regulator: National Highways Authority of India (NHAI)
Broader context: Motor Vehicles (Amendment) Act, 2019 — road safety reforms

title: Supreme Court Upholds Rs 8 Lakh EWS Income Limit; Rules Private Colleges Cannot Be Forced to Charge Government Fees
summary: The Supreme Court of India upheld the Rs 8 lakh per annum income limit for the Economically Weaker Section (EWS) category for reservation benefits. The court also ruled that private unaided medical colleges cannot be compelled by the government to charge fees at par with government institutions. This ruling reaffirms the distinction between government-aided and unaided private institutions in terms of regulatory control over fee structures. The judgment has implications for EWS reservation implementation across educational institutions.
exam_angle: EWS reservation (103rd Constitutional Amendment), fee regulation in private unaided institutions (Article 19(1)(g), TMA Pai Foundation case), and the Rs 8 lakh income threshold — key Polity topics.
key_facts: EWS income limit upheld: Rs 8 lakh per annum
103rd Constitutional Amendment: introduced 10% EWS reservation
SC ruling: private unaided colleges cannot be forced to charge government fee rates
Relevant precedent: TMA Pai Foundation case on autonomy of private institutions

title: Europe's 'Omega' Heatwave Sets Records; France Records Its Hottest Day Ever
summary: Europe is experiencing an extreme heatwave described as an 'Omega' blocking pattern — a high-pressure system shaped like the Greek letter Omega that traps heat over a region for extended periods. France recorded its hottest day ever during this event, with at least 40 people drowning across the country amid the crisis. The heatwave is attributed to amplified jet stream patterns linked to climate change. Scientists warn that such extreme heat events will become more frequent and intense under global warming scenarios.
exam_angle: Connects to climate change science, atmospheric blocking events, heat wave classification, and IPCC findings on extreme weather — GS Paper 3 (Environment) and GS Paper 1 (Geography).
key_facts: Heatwave type: 'Omega' blocking pattern (trapped high-pressure system)
France recorded its hottest day ever during the event
At least 40 drowning deaths in France linked to the crisis
Cause: amplified jet stream patterns associated with climate change

title: India–UAE Technology Pact Challenges Dominance of American Tech Giants
summary: India and the UAE have formalised a technology partnership agreement aimed at reducing reliance on dominant American technology companies in key digital infrastructure areas. The pact covers areas such as cloud computing, artificial intelligence, and digital public infrastructure. The agreement is seen as part of India's broader push for 'tech sovereignty' and aligns with the India–UAE Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (CEPA) signed in 2022. The partnership is expected to leverage UAE's financial resources and India's technology talent.
exam_angle: Tests knowledge of India–UAE bilateral relations, CEPA 2022, and India's digital sovereignty and technology diplomacy goals — GS Paper 2 (IR) and GS Paper 3 (Sci-Tech).
key_facts: India–UAE technology pact: covers AI, cloud, digital public infrastructure
Context: India–UAE CEPA signed in February 2022
Aim: reduce dependence on American tech platforms
Aligns with India's 'tech sovereignty' and Digital India goals

title: Meta's WhatsApp to Be Led by Indian Startup Founder Kunal Shah
summary: Meta has appointed Kunal Shah, the Indian entrepreneur best known as the founder of CRED (a fintech startup), to lead WhatsApp globally. This marks a significant appointment of an Indian-origin startup founder to head one of the world's most widely used messaging platforms with over 2 billion users globally. WhatsApp is particularly dominant in India, which is its largest market with approximately 500 million users. The appointment reflects the growing influence of Indian tech entrepreneurs on the global stage.
exam_angle: Relevant for 'Persons in News' and Sci-Tech sections — tests awareness of Indian entrepreneurs in global tech leadership; also connects to data privacy regulation (WhatsApp's compliance with India's Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023).
key_facts: Appointee: Kunal Shah, founder of CRED (Indian fintech)
New role: Head of WhatsApp (Meta's messaging platform)
WhatsApp users globally: 2+ billion; India is largest market (~500 million users)
Significance: Indian-origin tech founder heading a global Big Tech product

title: ED Searches Rajesh Exports Premises Over Suspected FEMA Violations
summary: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) conducted searches at the premises of Rajesh Exports — one of India's largest gold and jewellery export companies — in Bengaluru and Mumbai over suspected violations of the Foreign Exchange Management Act (FEMA). Investigators reportedly found stock mismatches and mega offshore deals during the search. The company, despite reporting revenues of Rs 7.7 lakh crore, allegedly had its CFO unpaid since 2020 and the MD receiving only Rs 17,000 per month, raising questions about corporate governance. FEMA violations can lead to penalties up to three times the amount involved.
exam_angle: Tests knowledge of FEMA (1999), the ED's mandate under FEMA and PMLA, and corporate governance norms — GS Paper 3 (Economy) and GS Paper 2 (Governance).
key_facts: Investigating agency: Enforcement Directorate (ED)
Company: Rajesh Exports (gold and jewellery exports)
Law violated: FEMA (Foreign Exchange Management Act, 1999)
Search locations: Bengaluru and Mumbai

title: Pew Research: Pakistan Outlier Among Muslim Nations; Pro-US Sentiment Rose During Iran Conflict
summary: A new Pew Research Center survey found that Pakistan stands out as an outlier among Muslim-majority countries, with pro-US sentiment having grown during the period of US–Iran military tensions. Most other Muslim-majority nations showed declining favourability toward the United States amid the Iran conflict. The finding is notable given Pakistan's complex and often strained relationship with the US, and its simultaneous close ties with China. The survey data provides insight into shifting public opinion across the Islamic world.
exam_angle: Relevant for GS Paper 2 (IR) — India's neighbourhood policy, Pakistan's foreign alignments, and West Asia geopolitics; also useful for essay and current events on global opinion polling.
key_facts: Source: Pew Research Center survey
Finding: Pakistan saw rising pro-US sentiment, unlike most Muslim-majority countries
Context: US–Iran military conflict period
Pakistan described as 'outlier' among Muslim-majority nations on US favourability
